buggy_pattern: old_path = args["old_path"]
fixed_pattern: old_path = args["path"]
sibling_location: FilesystemClaudeTextEditorMiddleware._handle_rename (~line 1054, same file) — identical KeyError on the same wrong key; the structural twin of StateClaudeTextEditorMiddleware._handle_rename (~line 555). Both were fixed in this PR; the sibling illustrates how a copy-pasted handler inherits the bug verbatim.

buggy_pattern: None => (self.0.trunc().to_string(), ""),
fixed_pattern: None => (num.as_str(), ""),
sibling_location: The Some arm of the same match in HumanFloatCount::fmt correctly takes int_str from the pre-formatted num string; the None arm (precision=0 / non-finite) was the unfixed sibling that used self.0.trunc() instead, silently discarding rounding (e.g. 1234.9 formatted at precision 0 rendered as "1,234" not "1,235"). Any future Human*Count formatter that replicates this split_once pattern is at risk.

buggy_pattern: return (opener.length + closer.length) % 3 != 0 or opener.length % 3 == 0 and closer.length % 3 == 0
fixed_pattern:
open_len = opener.orig_length
close_len = closer.orig_length
return (open_len + close_len) % 3 != 0 or open_len % 3 == 0 and close_len % 3 == 0
sibling_location: Any other location inside _finalize_emphasis_tokens or _can_match_emphasis_delimiters that reads opener.length / closer.length for CommonMark rules 9–10 without consulting orig_length; also any third-party mistune plugin that copies _can_match_emphasis_delimiters without the orig_length field.

buggy_pattern: const pathname = decodeURIComponent(url)
sibling_location: Other middleware files in packages/vite/src/node/server/middlewares/ (e.g., transformMiddleware.ts, staticMiddleware.ts) that call decodeURIComponent(url) on request paths without a try/catch; a malformed percent-encoded sequence (e.g., %gg) throws URIError and crashes the dev server.

buggy_pattern: let desired_width = config.term_width - longest - separator.len();
fixed_pattern:
let desired_width = config
    .term_width
    .saturating_sub(longest)
    .saturating_sub(separator.len());
sibling_location: print_snip function in the same codebase — the structurally identical usize underflow was fixed there first via saturating_sub in PR #3804. get_languages was the unfixed sibling; the CHANGELOG entry for #3812 explicitly calls out the mirror relationship.

buggy_pattern: if num_color_planes > 256 {
fixed_pattern: if spec == SpecCompliance::Strict && num_color_planes > 256 {
sibling_location: bits_per_pixel validation in read_entry (same function, ~10 lines later) — that check was already gated on SpecCompliance::Strict because bits_per_pixel doubles as a vertical hotspot coordinate in CUR files. num_color_planes (horizontal hotspot) was the unfixed structural sibling lacking the identical gate.

buggy_pattern:
let (min_x, max_x) = (edge_radius, width - edge_radius);
let (min_y, max_y) = (edge_radius, height - edge_radius);
sibling_location: The inner for-loops in the same function used the same unchecked subtraction in their range bounds (edge_radius..height - edge_radius / edge_radius..width - edge_radius); fixed to min_y..max_y / min_x..max_x in the same PR. Also: any other image-processing function in corners.rs or related modules that computes an interior region via u32 subtraction of a radius/border without saturating_sub.

buggy_pattern: if deviceInterface == nil || deviceInterface.HasDeviceRequest(task.Pod) { return nil, fmt.Errorf(...) }
sibling_location: AddBatchNodeOrderFn closure in same file (~line 213): bundled the nil check and HasDeviceRequest into one wrong compound condition, missing the reflect.ValueOf().IsNil() idiom for interface nil; AddPredicateFn closure already used the correct pattern and was the structural reference.

buggy_pattern: return value != null && value.equals(e);
fixed_pattern: return value != null && value.equals(e.getValue());
sibling_location: remove(Object o) method (~10 lines below) in the same EntrySet inner class already correctly compared against e.getValue(); contains(Object o) was the unfixed structural twin that compared against the Map.Entry object itself instead of its value.

buggy_pattern: return false  // in both validateValueWithStringPattern and validateValueWithIntPattern fallback branches when types do not match
fixed_pattern: return true  // types do not match => values ARE not-equal
sibling_location: pkg/engine/variables/operator/equal.go -- the source file from which notequal.go was copy-pasted; return false is correct in equal.go (types differ => not equal) but semantically inverted in notequal.go (types differ => ARE not-equal, so should return true).

buggy_pattern: MapSession session = event.getValue();  // in entryRemoved -- getValue() returns null for removal events
fixed_pattern: MapSession session = event.getOldValue();
sibling_location: entryAdded(EntryEvent<String, MapSession> event) ~line 267 in same file correctly uses event.getValue() (which returns the added value for add events); entryRemoved was the unfixed sibling that copy-pasted the accessor without adjusting for the different event semantics.

buggy_pattern: userId: auth0User?.hasura.id
sibling_location: web/scenes/Portal/Teams/page/index.tsx -- had identical missing ?. on hasura (const memberships = user.hasura.memberships with no optional chain); fixed in follow-up PR #1958 whose body explicitly calls it 'the same class of bug as #1957'.

buggy_pattern: if context.boundnode and isinstance(context.boundnode, bases.Instance):
sibling_location: if context and context.callcontext: guard a few lines below in _arguments_infer_argname -- the structurally parallel guard that already correctly checked context before accessing its attribute; the boundnode branch was the unfixed twin missing the outer context guard.

buggy_pattern: return CORE.Input.Gamepad.name[gamepad];
sibling_location: GetGamepadAxisCount(int gamepad) in same file -- fixed in this same PR with identical bounds guard; IsGamepadAvailable, IsGamepadButtonPressed/Down/Released/Up, GetGamepadAxisMovement all already guarded with gamepad < MAX_GAMEPADS and served as structural references.
ngx_stream_compile_complex_value() in src/stream/ngx_stream_script.c -- structurally identical function for stream protocol with the same missing i + 1 < v->len bounds guard; both fixed in this PR.

buggy_pattern: // missing: if err != nil { return err } at top of WalkFunc, swallowing fs.WalkDir errors silently
sibling_location: packageManifestLoader.LoadPackagesWalkFunc and packageManifestLoader.LoadBundleWalkFunc in pkg/manifests/packagemanifestloader.go -- both missing the same walk error guard; all three fixed together in PR #495.

buggy_pattern: // result_err_ty checks ty.is_diag_item(cx, sym::Result) without peeling async fn's impl Future<Output=T> wrapper first
sibling_location: result_unit_err lint check (same file, same result_err_ty helper) -- PR author notes 'I also found that result_unit_err also had the same problem' in the PR body; both result_large_err and result_unit_err shared the same helper that silently skipped async functions.

buggy_pattern: callWithErrorHandling(ref, owner, ErrorCodes.FUNCTION_REF, [value, refs])
sibling_location: packages/runtime-vapor/src/apiTemplateRef.ts -- parallel package implementing same template ref system for Vapor renderer; had identical missing pauseTracking/resetTracking wrapping; fixed in follow-up PR #14986 whose title explicitly mirrors #14985.

buggy_pattern: restrictedArea($user, 'fournisseur', $object, 'commande_fournisseur', 'commande')
fixed_pattern: restrictedArea($user, 'fournisseur', $object->origin_id, 'commande_fournisseur', 'commande')
sibling_location: htdocs/reception/contact.php -- identical wrong-object bug (passing $object instead of $object->origin_id to restrictedArea), fixed in the same PR; htdocs/reception/card.php already used $object->origin_id correctly and served as the reference the PR explicitly cites.

buggy_pattern: # TYPE patroni_xlog_location counter\n# HELP patroni_xlog_location 0 if this node is not the leader
fixed_pattern: # TYPE patroni_postgres_timeline gauge  (timeline CAN decrease, so gauge is correct)
sibling_location: api.py lines 661/683/689 pre-fix: patroni_xlog_location, patroni_xlog_received_location, patroni_xlog_replayed_location still declare '# TYPE ... counter' even though xlog positions can decrease on timeline change

buggy_pattern: cp := Context{writermem: c.writermem, Request: c.Request, engine: c.engine} // sameSite field never set in Copy()
fixed_pattern: cp.Errors = make(errorMsgs, len(c.Errors)); copy(cp.Errors, c.Errors); cp.Accepted = make([]string, len(c.Accepted)); copy(cp.Accepted, c.Accepted)
sibling_location: context.go: Errors and Accepted fields got deep-copy logic; sameSite http.SameSite field (line 96 of struct) is still not copied in Copy() — left at zero value

buggy_pattern: if (!Character.isWhitespace(str.charAt(i))) { chs[count++] = str.charAt(i); }  // in deleteWhitespace(), char-based, misses supplementary code points
fixed_pattern: getDigits() changed from charAt(i) to codePointAt(i) iteration with Character.isDigit(codePoint) and code-point-aware index advancement
sibling_location: StringUtils.java pre-fix lines 1654-1655: deleteWhitespace() still uses str.charAt(i) iteration (same supplementary-codepoint bug as the fixed getDigits())

buggy_pattern: def startElement(self, name, attrs): super().startElement(name, sorted_attrs)  // no control-char check on attribute values
fixed_pattern: startElement() now validates each attribute value with re.search(r'[\x00-\x08\x0B-\x0C\x0E-\x1F]', v) before calling super()
sibling_location: xmlutils.py characters() already had the re.search control-char guard; startElement() was the unfixed sibling that passed unvalidated attr values to the parent handler

buggy_pattern: trimmedArgs = args[:len(args)-1]  // slice alias in getFlagCompletionArgs(), shares backing array
fixed_pattern: trimmedArgs := make([]string, len(args)-1); copy(trimmedArgs, args[:len(args)-1])  // in line-320 callsite
sibling_location: completions.go line 713 in getFlagCompletionArgs(): trimmedArgs = args[:len(args)-1] still creates alias without copy — same pattern as the fixed site at line 320

buggy_pattern: case OPENSSL: return new OpenSslServerContext(..., resumptionController, ctxOptions)  // missing 'credentials' arg in newServerContextInternal
fixed_pattern: case OPENSSL: return new OpenSslServerContext(..., resumptionController, ctxOptions, credentials)
sibling_location: SslContext.java newServerContextInternal OPENSSL_REFCNT branch already passed credentials correctly; OPENSSL branch was the leftover missing credentials

buggy_pattern: SslProvider provider = (serverName != null) ? this.serverNameSslProviders.get(serverName) : this.sslProvider;  // get() returns null for unmapped serverName -> NPE
fixed_pattern: this.serverNameSslProviders.getOrDefault(serverName, this.sslProvider)
sibling_location: SslServerCustomizer.getSslProvider() in spring-boot-reactor-netty module already used getOrDefault correctly; HttpServerSslCustomizer.applySecurity() in RSocket server was the leftover

buggy_pattern: if sub == "" || strings.HasPrefix(sub, ".") || strings.Contains(sub, "..") { return false }  // missing trailing-dot check
fixed_pattern: added strings.HasSuffix(sub, ".") || to catch trailing-dot labels like 'foo.example.com.'
sibling_location: utils.go subdomain.match(): strings.HasPrefix(sub, ".") (leading-dot guard) was already present; strings.HasSuffix trailing-dot guard was the missing sibling

buggy_pattern: if rr.Do() { if rr.Co() { s += "flags: do, co; " } else { s += "flags: do; " } } else { s += "flags:; " }  // Co() never checked when Do()=false
fixed_pattern: independent checks: if rr.Do() { s += " do" }; if rr.Co() { s += " co" }
sibling_location: edns.go OPT.String(): Do() flag was shown correctly in both branches; Co() flag was only shown when Do()=true — the Do()=false else-branch was the unfixed sibling

buggy_pattern: elif isinstance(fp, _SupportsRead):  // in _encode_files(), bare isinstance without hasattr fallback
fixed_pattern: elif isinstance(fp, _SupportsRead) or hasattr(fp, 'read'):
sibling_location: models.py _encode_params() at line 164 pre-fix still used bare isinstance(data, _SupportsRead) without hasattr fallback — same pattern as the fixed _encode_files() site

buggy_pattern: public long skip(final long n) throws IOException { charsRead += n; return super.skip(n); }  // no bound check, over-increments even if fewer skipped
fixed_pattern: checks remaining = maxCharsFromTargetReader - charsRead; skips min(n, remaining); adds only actual skipped count
sibling_location: BoundedReader.java read() at lines 87-93 correctly checked bounds and incremented charsRead by 1 per char; skip() was the leftover without equivalent accounting

buggy_pattern: readLine() has no incPos() calls — char position tracking missing for line-terminator characters consumed
fixed_pattern: added final int startPos = bufPos; and incPos(bufPos - startPos) calls at both LF and CR return points in readLine()
sibling_location: UnsynchronizedBufferedReader.java: single-char read() called incPos(1) and read(char[],int,int) called incPos(count); readLine() was the only read variant without incPos tracking

buggy_pattern: if (prop == null) { p.skipChildren(); continue; }  // in _deserializeUsingPropertyBased(), no FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES check on extra array elements
fixed_pattern: added: if (i >= propCount && !_ignoreAllUnknown && ctxt.isEnabled(DeserializationFeature.FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES)) { ctxt.reportWrongTokenException(...); }
sibling_location: BeanAsArrayDeserializer.java deserialize() at line 286 pre-fix: non-creator path already had 'if (!_ignoreAllUnknown && ctxt.isEnabled(FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES))' check after extra elements loop; creator path _deserializeUsingPropertyBased() was the leftover

buggy_pattern: func Stringer(key string, val fmt.Stringer) Field { return Field{Key: key, Type: zapcore.StringerType, Interface: val} }  // no nil check, panics when encoder calls val.String()
fixed_pattern: func Object(key string, val zapcore.ObjectMarshaler) Field { if val == nil { return nilField(key) }; return Field{...} }
sibling_location: field.go: Object() was fixed with nil guard; Stringer() (same pattern: takes interface, stores directly) has no nil guard and still panics on nil in current HEAD

buggy_pattern: histograms = histograms[:len(histograms)-drop]; ev.currentSamples -= totalHPointSize(histograms)  // called AFTER rotation, subtracts remaining not dropped histograms
fixed_pattern: droppedSize := totalHPointSize(histograms[:drop])  // captured BEFORE rotation; ev.currentSamples -= droppedSize
sibling_location: engine.go matrixIterSlice() float overlap case: ev.currentSamples -= drop (before slice trim) was correct; histogram overlap case used totalHPointSize after slice trim — accounting for wrong set

buggy_pattern: node.callee.name === "Promise" &&
fixed_pattern: node.callee.name === "Promise" && sourceCode.isGlobalReference(node.callee) &&
sibling_location: lib/rules/prefer-promise-reject-errors.js line ~63: `callExpression.arguments[0].name === "undefined"` without `isGlobalReference` check — shadowed `undefined` path fixed later by PR #21006 the same day (merged 11:13 UTC vs 04:41 UTC)

buggy_pattern: rejectionReason.name === "undefined"
fixed_pattern: rejectionReason.name === "undefined" && sourceCode.isGlobalReference(rejectionReason)
sibling_location: lib/rules/no-throw-literal.js line ~36: `node.argument.name === "undefined"` without `isGlobalReference` — still unfixed when this PR merged (June 22 11:13 UTC); fixed by PR #21010 at 14:53 UTC same day

buggy_pattern: node.argument.name === "undefined"
fixed_pattern: node.argument.name === "undefined" && sourceCode.isGlobalReference(node.argument)
sibling_location: lib/rules/radix.js function isValidRadix: `radix.type === "Identifier" && radix.name === "undefined"` with no `isGlobalReference` call — unfixed when this PR merged (June 22 14:53 UTC); fixed by PR #21011 at 15:21 UTC

buggy_pattern: radix.type === "Identifier" && radix.name === "undefined"
fixed_pattern: radix.type === "Identifier" && radix.name === "undefined" && sourceCode.isGlobalReference(radix)
sibling_location: lib/rules/no-extra-boolean-cast.js: two call-sites checking `node.callee.name === "Boolean"` without `isGlobalReference` — unfixed when this PR merged (June 22 15:21 UTC); fixed by PR #21013 at 18:39 UTC

buggy_pattern: if (parent.type !== "CallExpression") { return; } callbackStack.push(node);
fixed_pattern: if (parent.type !== "CallExpression" || parent.callee === node) { return; } callbackStack.push(node);
sibling_location: lib/rules/max-nested-callbacks.js same checkFunction: PR #20979 (created June 12, open when this PR merged June 18) added `|| parent.callee === node` to exclude IIFEs from callback count — the sibling IIFE exclusion was in an open PR not yet merged

buggy_pattern: callWithErrorHandling(ref, instance, ErrorCodes.FUNCTION_REF, [value, refs])
fixed_pattern: pauseTracking(); try { callWithErrorHandling(...) } finally { resetTracking() }
sibling_location: packages/runtime-core/src/rendererTemplateRef.ts setRef() function: identical missing pauseTracking/resetTracking around function-ref invocation — PR #14985 was created minutes later but not merged until June 25 (7 days after this PR merged June 18)

buggy_pattern: case ir.OpKind.Property: case ir.OpKind.Attribute: /* apply sanitizer */
fixed_pattern: case ir.OpKind.Property: case ir.OpKind.Attribute: case ir.OpKind.TwoWayProperty: /* apply sanitizer */
sibling_location: resolve_sanitizers.ts same switch: ir.OpKind.TwoWayProperty (two-way binding [()]) was not covered by sanitizer dispatch — dangerous properties like innerHTML/srcdoc/src stayed unsanitized in two-way bindings; fixed 12 days later by PR #69107

buggy_pattern: if ($this->isJsonSelector($where['column']))
fixed_pattern: if ($this->isJsonSelector($column))
sibling_location: Same file whereTime() method at line ~130: identical `$this->isJsonSelector($where['column'])` instead of `$this->isJsonSelector($column)` — both whereDate and whereTime were fixed in 13.x branch (May 29), but 12.x branch had the same bug in both methods; fixed 21 days later by PR #60540

buggy_pattern: @param  array{0: 'strict'}  $parameters
fixed_pattern: @param  array{0?: 'strict'}  $parameters
sibling_location: Same file validateConfirmed() at line ~507: `@param array{0: string} $parameters` — first parameter is optional but docblock marks it required; unfixed when PR #60549 merged (June 19 20:46); fixed the next day by PR #60553 (June 20 22:13)

buggy_pattern: foreach ((new \ReflectionClass($targetClass))->getProperties() as $reflProperty)
fixed_pattern: foreach ($this->getAllProperties(new \ReflectionClass($targetClass)) as $reflProperty)
sibling_location: src/Symfony/Component/ObjectMapper/Metadata/ReflectionObjectMapperMetadataFactory.php: already used ClassHierarchyTrait and getAllProperties() to include parent-class properties — the reverse mapper was the sibling that missed the trait, causing private properties from parent classes to be invisible

buggy_pattern: if typeDeclaration.keyword == "enum", typeDeclaration.hasModifier("indirect") { return }
fixed_pattern: if typeDeclaration.keyword == "enum" { if typeDeclaration.hasModifier("indirect") { return } if typeDeclaration.body.contains(where: { $0.keyword == "case" && $0.hasModifier("indirect") }) { return } }
sibling_location: Same function in RedundantSendable.swift: enum with individual `indirect case` members (e.g. `enum Foo { indirect case bar(Foo) }`) was NOT protected — only top-level `indirect enum` was guarded; the case-level indirect variant was left vulnerable to incorrect Sendable removal for 50 days until PR #2564 (June 26)

buggy_pattern: /* no override for TypeExprSyntax in cast position */
fixed_pattern: override func visit(_ node: TypeExprSyntax) -> SyntaxVisitorContinueKind { if case .likeClass = parentDeclScopes.peek(), isCastOperand(node) { return .skipChildren }; return .visitChildren }
sibling_location: Same visitor class: TypeExprSyntax used as the type operand of is/as/as?/as! casts was not skipped — `x is A` would incorrectly rewrite to `x is Self` changing runtime semantics; PR #6749 added skips for CompositionTypeSyntax/SomeOrAnyTypeSyntax/MetatypeTypeSyntax but missed cast operands; fixed 3 days later by PR #6765

buggy_pattern: Instant.fromEpochSeconds(value.time / 1000, value.nanos)
fixed_pattern: Instant.fromEpochSeconds(Math.floorDiv(value.time, 1000L), value.nanos)
sibling_location: exposed-core/src/main/kotlin/org/jetbrains/exposed/v1/core/datetime/LocalDateTimeColumnType.kt line ~84: `longToLocalDateTime(value.time / 1000, value.nanos.toLong())` — identical truncating division for pre-epoch Timestamps (negative millis / 1000 truncates toward zero instead of flooring); InstantColumnType fixed May 6 2026; LocalDateTimeColumnType unfixed as of batch-3 mining date

buggy_pattern: stream.ReadExactly(eocdBlock._archiveComment)
fixed_pattern: await stream.ReadExactlyAsync(eocdBlock._archiveComment, cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false)
sibling_location: src/libraries/System.IO.Compression/src/System/IO/Compression/ZipBlocks.Async.cs ReadBlockAsync(): `stream.ReadExactly(eocdBlock._archiveComment)` — the async overload of ReadBlockAsync was calling the synchronous ReadExactly for the archive comment, blocking the calling thread; PR #121938 (Dec 2025) fixed many sync calls but missed this one; fixed 6 months later by PR #129122 (June 2026)

buggy_pattern: callWithErrorHandling(ref, owner, ErrorCodes.FUNCTION_REF, [value, refs])
fixed_pattern: pauseTracking(); try { callWithErrorHandling(ref, owner, ErrorCodes.FUNCTION_REF, [value, refs]) } finally { resetTracking() }
sibling_location: packages/runtime-vapor/src/apiTemplateRef.ts setRef() helper: identical missing pauseTracking guard around function-ref invocations — runtime-vapor version was already fixed (PR #14986 merged June 18); runtime-core version (#14985) was created same day but sat unmerged until June 25, so at the time of vapor fix the core sibling was openly known-buggy and tracked in a pending PR

buggy_pattern: return nil, err (raw gRPC status error returned to caller without conversion)
fixed_pattern: return nil, errgrpc.ToNative(err)
sibling_location: core/content/proxy/content_reader.go — same raw gRPC err return pattern; errgrpc.ToNative conversion was missing; fixed 4 days later in PR #13585 (2026-06-19)

buggy_pattern: // cut(): passes raw Series with NaN directly to map_enum_cats() with no NaN-to-null conversion
fixed_pattern: // qcut(): let ca = ca.set(&ca.is_nan(), None)?; // NaN → null before quantile computation
sibling_location: cut() function in same file: no ca.is_nan() call before categorical mapping; NaN values silently propagate into output categories

buggy_pattern: #[derive(Default)] pub(crate) specular_factor: Option<f64>  // Default → None; raw extension.get("specularFactor").and_then(Value::as_f64)
fixed_pattern: pub(crate) specular_factor: f32  // explicit impl Default { fn default() { specular_factor: 1.0 } }; parsed via typed specular.specular_factor() from gltf crate
sibling_location: crates/bevy_gltf/src/loader/extensions/khr_materials_clearcoat.rs: ClearcoatExtension still uses #[derive(Default)] with Option<f64> fields and raw serde_json::Value parsing for clearcoatFactor/clearcoatRoughnessFactor

buggy_pattern: let past_seqlen = total_seqlen - uniforms.global_shape[1];  // u32 underflow when total_seqlen < seqlen for right-padded sequences
fixed_pattern: let past_seqlen = select(total_seqlen - uniforms.global_shape[1], 0u, total_seqlen <= uniforms.global_shape[1]);
sibling_location: onnxruntime/core/providers/cpu/nn/attention_helper/gqa_attention_base.h: size_t seq_causal_length = causal_past_seqlen + seq + 1 overflows for padding rows → reads uninitialized memory; fixed in PR#29069 one day later

buggy_pattern: Py_DECREF(ap_c);  // decref's the sliced array being returned instead of the full temporary buffer
fixed_pattern: Py_DECREF(ap_c_full);  // decref the temporary ap_c_full; ap_c is the return value and must not be freed
sibling_location: fitpack_curfit() and fitpack_percur() in same file: both missing Py_DECREF(ap_wrk) and Py_DECREF(ap_iwrk) on temporary work buffers — same ref-count management bug family, fixed 2 days later in PR#25275
